title: Hot and Sour Soup

Ingredients for Hot and Sour Soup

Tips for the Best Hot and Sour Soup
seo.imageAlt: A steaming bowl of authentic Chinese Hot and Sour Soup with tofu, mushrooms, bamboo shoots, and a sprinkle of green onions.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a comforting bowl of homemade Hot and Sour Soup, a classic Chinese favorite with a perfect balance of spicy and tangy flavors.

description: Experience the vibrant flavors of a classic Chinese Hot and Sour Soup, a comforting bowl that perfectly balances fiery spice with a delightful tangy kick. This traditional soup is packed with an array of textures from silky tofu, tender mushrooms, and crisp bamboo shoots, making every spoonful a culinary adventure. It is a beloved staple, offering warmth and a burst of complex flavors that tantalize the palate.

ingredients.notes: mixed with 3 tbsp cold water to form a slurry

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Rehydrate dried shiitake and wood ear mushrooms in separate bowls of hot water for at least 20 minutes. Once soft, squeeze out excess water, remove stems from shiitake, and slice both types of mushrooms thinly. Cut tofu and bamboo shoots into matchsticks. Shred pork shoulder thinly. Prepare cornstarch slurry and beat eggs.

instructions.title: Cook Pork and Build Broth
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, bring chicken broth to a boil. Add the shredded pork and cook until it changes color, about 2-3 minutes. Skim off any foam that rises to the surface. Add the sliced shiitake mushrooms, wood ear mushrooms, and bamboo shoots to the broth.

instructions.title: Season the Soup
instructions.text: Stir in the soy sauce, Chinkiang vinegar, and white pepper. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed, adding a pinch of salt if desired. The soup should have a balanced hot and sour flavor.

instructions.title: Thicken the Soup
instructions.text: Bring the soup back to a gentle boil. Slowly stir in the cornstarch slurry a little at a time, stirring constantly, until the soup reaches your desired thickness. Let it simmer for 1-2 minutes to cook out the raw cornstarch taste.

instructions.title: Add Eggs and Tofu
instructions.text: While the soup is simmering, slowly pour the beaten eggs into the soup in a thin stream, stirring gently in one direction with a spoon or chopsticks to create delicate egg ribbons. Immediately add the tofu matchsticks.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the soup from heat. Stir in the sesame oil. Ladle the hot soup into bowls. Garnish with sliced green onions and a drizzle of chili oil, if using. Serve immediately.

tips: For a truly authentic taste, use good quality Chinkiang vinegar; its depth of flavor is key to the soup's sourness. Do not substitute with regular white vinegar if possible.
If wood ear mushrooms are unavailable, fresh shiitake mushrooms can be used, though the texture will be slightly different. For a vegetarian version, omit pork and use vegetable broth.
Hot and Sour Soup is best served immediately, but leftovers can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 2-3 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop and adjust seasoning if needed.
article:
Hot and Sour Soup is more than just a dish; it's a culinary adventure in a bowl, a vibrant tapestry of flavors and textures that dance on the palate. Originating from the rich culinary traditions of China, this iconic soup has captivated food lovers worldwide with its unique blend of savory, spicy, and tangy notes. It’s a testament to the sophisticated balance that Chinese cuisine often achieves, offering warmth, comfort, and an invigorating kick all at once. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a home cook eager to explore Asian flavors, understanding and mastering the Hot and Sour Soup Recipe is a rewarding journey. This article will guide you through everything you need to know about this beloved classic, from its essential ingredients to expert cooking tips and delightful serving suggestions.

## What is Hot and Sour Soup?
At its core, Hot and Sour Soup is a thick, savory, and immensely flavorful broth characterized by its distinctive "hot" (spicy) and "sour" (tangy) profile. The heat typically comes from white pepper and, optionally, chili oil, while the sourness is primarily derived from rice vinegar. Beyond these signature flavors, the soup boasts a complex umami depth from ingredients like soy sauce, mushrooms, and a rich stock. Its texture is also a key element, thickened with cornstarch to a silky consistency and often featuring delicate strands of egg swirled throughout. Common additions include shredded pork, firm tofu, bamboo shoots, and various types of mushrooms, such as shiitake and wood ear, providing a delightful chewiness and earthy notes that complement the broth beautifully. It’s a popular appetizer in Chinese restaurants globally, but its hearty nature also makes it a satisfying light meal.

## Ingredients for Hot and Sour Soup
Crafting an authentic Hot and Sour Soup Recipe begins with assembling the right components. Quality ingredients are paramount to achieving that perfect balance of flavors.

**For the Broth Base:**
*   **Chicken or Vegetable Broth:** 6-8 cups. A good quality broth is the foundation.
*   **Water:** If needed, to adjust consistency.

**Proteins & Vegetables:**
*   **Pork:** 4-6 oz, lean pork loin or shoulder, shredded thinly (optional, but traditional).
*   **Firm Tofu:** 4-6 oz, pressed and cut into small strips or cubes.
*   **Dried Shiitake Mushrooms:** 6-8, rehydrated, stems removed, and sliced thinly.
*   **Dried Wood Ear Mushrooms:** 1/4 cup, rehydrated and sliced thinly (adds a unique crunch).
*   **Bamboo Shoots:** 1/2 cup, canned, rinsed, and sliced thinly.
*   **Lily Flowers (Dried Golden Needles):** 1/4 cup, rehydrated and tied into knots (optional, but adds authentic flavor and texture).
*   **Green Onions:** 2-3 stalks, sliced for garnish.
*   **Cilantro:** A handful, chopped for garnish (optional).

**Flavorings & Seasonings:**
*   **Soy Sauce (Light):** 3-4 tablespoons.
*   **Dark Soy Sauce:** 1 teaspoon (for color, optional).
*   **Rice Vinegar:** 3-5 tablespoons (adjust to taste for sourness).
*   **White Pepper:** 1-2 teaspoons, freshly ground if possible (crucial for the "hot" element).
*   **Sesame Oil:** 1-2 teaspoons, for finishing.
*   **Chili Oil or Chili Paste:** 1-2 teaspoons (optional, for extra heat).
*   **Salt:** To taste.

**Thickener & Egg:**
*   **Cornstarch Slurry:** 3-4 tablespoons cornstarch mixed with 4-5 tablespoons cold water.
*   **Eggs:** 2 large, lightly beaten.

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Hot and Sour Soup
Mastering the Hot and Sour Soup Recipe at home is surprisingly straightforward. Follow these steps for a delicious bowl:

1.  **Prepare Ingredients:** If using dried mushrooms and lily flowers, soak them in hot water for at least 30 minutes until soft. Drain, squeeze out excess water, remove shiitake stems, and slice all thinly. If using pork, slice it into thin strips. Press tofu to remove excess water and slice. Prepare your cornstarch slurry. Lightly beat the eggs.
2.  **Cook Pork (Optional):** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat a tablespoon of oil over medium-high heat. Add the shredded pork and stir-fry until cooked through, about 2-3 minutes. Remove pork and set aside.
3.  **Combine Broth & Vegetables:** Pour the chicken or vegetable broth into the pot. Add the sliced shiitake mushrooms, wood ear mushrooms, bamboo shoots, and lily flowers (if using). Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
4.  **Add Flavorings:** Stir in the soy sauce (light and dark), rice vinegar, and white pepper. Taste and adjust the sourness and spiciness to your preference. This is where you really define your Hot and Sour Soup Recipe.
5.  **Thicken the Soup:** Give the cornstarch slurry a quick re-stir, then slowly pour it into the simmering soup in a thin stream while continuously stirring. Continue stirring until the soup thickens to your desired consistency. If it's too thick, add a little more broth or water; if too thin, make a bit more slurry.
6.  **Add Egg & Tofu:** Once the soup is thickened, reduce the heat to low. Slowly drizzle the beaten eggs into the soup in a thin stream while gently stirring in one direction with chopsticks or a fork. This creates beautiful egg "flowers" or ribbons. Immediately add the sliced tofu and the cooked pork (if using) back into the pot.
7.  **Finish & Serve:** Remove the soup from the heat. Stir in the sesame oil and, if desired, chili oil or paste. Taste one last time and adjust salt, pepper, or vinegar as needed. Ladle into bowls and garnish generously with fresh green onions and cilantro.

## Tips for the Best Hot and Sour Soup
Achieving that perfect balance of flavors and textures in your Hot and Sour Soup Recipe requires a few insider tricks:

*   **Balance is Key:** The "hot" and "sour" elements are subjective. Start with the recommended amounts of white pepper and vinegar, then gradually add more until it suits your palate. Remember, you can always add more, but you can't take it away.
*   **Fresh White Pepper:** Using freshly ground white pepper makes a noticeable difference in the soup's aromatic heat.
*   **Don't Over-Thicken:** The soup should be silky, not gloopy. Add the cornstarch slurry gradually and stir constantly. If it becomes too thick, a splash of hot broth or water can thin it out.
*   **Thin Slicing:** Uniformly thin slices of mushrooms, bamboo shoots, and pork ensure even cooking and a pleasant mouthfeel.
*   **Rehydrate Thoroughly:** Ensure dried ingredients like shiitake and wood ear mushrooms are fully rehydrated and soft before slicing and adding to the soup for the best texture and flavor.
*   **Egg Technique:** Drizzle the beaten egg slowly while stirring gently in a circular motion to create delicate, feathery strands rather than large clumps.
*   **Let it Rest:** A few minutes of resting after cooking allows the flavors to meld further before serving.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings
Hot and Sour Soup is incredibly versatile. It shines as a standalone appetizer, particularly on a chilly day, but also complements a wide array of Chinese dishes.

*   **As an Appetizer:** Serve hot, garnished with extra white pepper, a swirl of chili oil, and fresh green onions or cilantro.
*   **Light Meal:** Pair it with a simple bowl of steamed white rice or a few potstickers for a satisfying lunch.
*   **Part of a Feast:** It's an excellent prelude to heavier main courses like General Tso's Chicken, Beef with Broccoli, or a vegetable stir-fry.
*   **Beverage Pairings:** A light, crisp lager or a refreshing green tea can cut through the richness and spice, cleansing the palate between spoonfuls.

## Variations & Dietary Adaptations
One of the beauties of a classic like the Hot and Sour Soup Recipe is its adaptability.

*   **Vegetarian/Vegan:** Easily make this soup vegetarian or vegan by using vegetable broth and omitting the pork. Increase the amount of tofu and mushrooms for more substance. Ensure your soy sauce is vegan-friendly (some contain alcohol or fish derivatives).
*   **Gluten-Free:** Substitute tamari for regular soy sauce and ensure your broth is certified gluten-free.
*   **Spice Level:** For more heat, add extra white pepper, a dash of hot sauce, or more chili oil. For less spice, reduce the white pepper.
*   **Protein Alternatives:** Instead of pork, you can use thinly sliced chicken breast, shrimp, or even ground chicken or turkey.
*   **Additional Vegetables:** Feel free to incorporate other thinly sliced vegetables like carrots, water chestnuts, or snow peas for added color, texture, and nutrients.
*   **Richness:** For an even richer soup, some variations might include a dash of chicken fat or a richer homemade stock.

Armed with this comprehensive Hot and Sour Soup Recipe guide, you're ready to create a truly memorable bowl of this Chinese classic. Its complexity of flavor, comforting warmth, and invigorating kick make it a perennial favorite. Don't be intimidated by the number of ingredients; each plays a vital role in creating a symphony of tastes. Experiment with the "hot" and "sour" elements to find your perfect balance, and enjoy the delicious journey of bringing this iconic soup to your own kitchen.
